history.content:
<thought>
I am at (1,1) facing right. The cell directly ahead is lava, so I cannot move forward. I need to head downward to eventually reach the goal at (7,7). Turning right will face me down, allowing me to move south along the left column (col1) which is clear until row5.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing down at (1,1). The cell directly below is empty (row2 col1), so I can move forward. Moving down will bring me closer to the goal, as I need to reach the bottom area. I will continue moving south until I hit an obstacle or need to turn.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I moved down one cell. Now I am at row3 col1, still facing down. The cell directly below (row4 col1) is '.' (empty), so I can continue moving downward to approach the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I moved down again. Now I am at row4 col1. The cell directly below (row5 col1) is '.' (empty), so I continue moving downward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD</action>

<thought>
Currently I am at (row4, col1) facing down (`v`). The cell directly in front (row5, col1) is floor, so moving forward is safe and brings me down to the open row. From there I can turn left to head east toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row5, col1, facing down. The cell directly in front (row6, col1) is lava (#). To avoid the lava and head east toward the goal, I should turn left to face east. Then I can move forward along the safe row5 all the way to col7, then turn right to face down and move to the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row5 col1 facing east. The row ahead has safe floor cells all the way to col7. I can move forward along this row, then turn right to face south, then move down to (row6, col7) and then to the goal at (row7, col7). The next step is to move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row5 col2 facing east. The path ahead is clear floor until the end of the row. I need to reach the goal which is at the bottom row, second column from the right. I should continue moving forward along this row.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row5, col3 facing east. The row ahead has clear floor all the way to col7. I need to reach the easternmost part of this row to then turn south and go to the goal. So I will continue moving forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row5 col4 facing east. The path ahead is still clear. I need to reach the right side of this row to be able to descend toward the goal in the bottom-right. Continue moving forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row5 col5 facing east. The next cell east is empty, so I can continue moving forward toward the right edge to then turn south toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row5 col6 facing east. The next cell to the east is empty (row5 col7). Moving forward will bring me closer to the right edge, from where I can turn south and proceed down to the goal at the bottom-right.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row5 col7 facing east. The next cell east is a wall (#), so I cannot move forward. The goal is directly south at (7,7). I need to turn right to face south, then move forward twice to reach the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row5 col7 facing south. The cell directly south is empty floor. Moving forward will bring me to row6 col7, one step closer to the goal at row7 col7.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am at row6 col7 facing south. The cell directly south is the goal (G) at row7 col7. Moving forward will reach the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
